from dascore.core import annotationsfrom dascore.core import annotations---
title: Inventory For A Complex Deployment
execute:
  warning: false
---

This recipe follows a trenched DAS deployment through several metadata updates:

1. A trench with three linear fiber segments starts acquisition on June 1, 2026.
2. The acquisition parameters are corrected after the first inventory was written.
3. In August, the team learns that a fiber break occurred and replaces the optical path interpretation.
4. After a splice repair, the optical path is replaced with the repaired interpretation.

The important pattern is that the patch points at one `data_source_id` for inventory context, while inventory objects are edited by extracting them from `Inventory.networks`, calling `.revise(...)`, and writing them back with `Inventory.replace(...)`.

## Part 1: Create The Initial Inventory

Start with a simple map: four trench vertices define three straight fiber segments.

```python
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import dascore as dc
from dascore.core import inventory as inv

trench_vertices = np.array(
    [
        [0.0, 0.0, 0.0],
        [100.0, 0.0, 0.0],
        [100.0, 80.0, 0.0],
        [220.0, 80.0, 0.0],
    ]
)
segment_lengths = np.linalg.norm(np.diff(trench_vertices, axis=0), axis=1)
distance_edges = np.concatenate([[0.0], np.cumsum(segment_lengths)])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 3))
ax.plot(trench_vertices[:, 0], trench_vertices[:, 1], marker="o")
for index, (x, y, _) in enumerate(trench_vertices):
    ax.text(x, y + 4, f"P{index}")
ax.set_title("Three-segment trenched fiber")
ax.set_xlabel("x (m)")
ax.set_ylabel("y (m)")
ax.set_aspect("equal", adjustable="box")
```

Now create items for the interrogator, acquisition settings, fiber segments, geometry, coupling, annotations, and the patch-resolvable fiber array.

```python
interrogator = inv.Interrogator(
    resource_id="trench_interrogator",
    manufacturer="Example Instruments",
    model="DAS-1000",
    serial_number="EX-2026-01",
)

acquisition = inv.Acquisition(
    code="RAW",
    location_code="00",
    data_category="DAS",
    data_type="strain",
    data_units="strain",
    interrogator=interrogator,
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    acquisition_sample_rate=500.0,
    gauge_length=10.0,
    pulse_rate=10_000.0,
    spatial_sampling_interval=1.0,
    first_channel_distance=0.0,
)

crs = inv.CoordinateReferenceSystem(
    resource_id="trench_local_crs",
    authority="LOCAL",
    code="trench_local_cartesian",
    axis_order=("x", "y", "z"),
    units="m",
)

fibers = tuple(
    inv.FiberSegment(
        name=f"Trench fiber segment {index}",
        optical_length=float(length),
        fiber_type="single_mode",
    )
    for index, length in enumerate(segment_lengths, start=1)
)
geometries = tuple(
    inv.Geometry(
        optical_length=float(length),
        geometry_type="linear",
        coordinates=(tuple(start), tuple(stop)),
    )
    for index, (length, start, stop) in enumerate(
        zip(segment_lengths, trench_vertices[:-1], trench_vertices[1:]),
        start=1,
    )
)
coupling = tuple(
    inv.CouplingCondition(
        optical_length=float(length),
        coupling_type="buried",
        deployment_type="trench",
        quality="unknown",
    )
    for index, length in enumerate(segment_lengths, start=1)
)
annotations = tuple(
    annotations.OpticalPathAnnotation(
        distance=(float(start), float(stop)),
        label=f"trench_{index}",
        category="segment",
    )
    for index, (start, stop) in enumerate(
        zip(distance_edges[:-1], distance_edges[1:]),
        start=1,
    )
)

optical_path = inv.OpticalPath(
    name="Trench optical path",
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    optical_length=float(distance_edges[-1]),
    coordinate_reference_system=crs,
    optical_components=fibers,
    geometries=geometries,
    coupling_conditions=coupling,
    annotations=annotations,
).validate()

fiber_array = inv.FiberArray(
    code="TRN",
    name="Three-segment trench array",
    start_time="2026-06-01",
    acquisitions=(acquisition,),
    optical_paths=(optical_path,),
    dims="distance,time",
    tag="trench",
)

network = inv.Network(
    code="XX",
    name="Example trench network",
    description="Example three-segment trench deployment.",
    fiber_arrays=(fiber_array,),
)

inventory = inv.Inventory(
    resource_id="trench_inventory",
    networks=(network,),
)

print(inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0])
```

## Part 2: Correct Acquisition Parameters

Later, the team finds the acquisition settings in the first inventory were wrong. Replace the acquisition by revising the extracted object and reinserting it.

```python
resolved_fiber_array = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0]
resolved_acquisition = resolved_fiber_array.acquisitions[0]
corrected_acquisition = resolved_acquisition.revise(
    acquisition_sample_rate=250.0,
    spatial_sampling_interval=1.25,
    comment="Corrected from field notes after initial inventory creation.",
)

inventory = inventory.replace(corrected_acquisition)

resolved = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0]

print(resolved.acquisitions[0].acquisition_sample_rate)
print(resolved.acquisitions[0].spatial_sampling_interval)
```

## Part 3: Add A Retrospective Fiber Break

In August, the team discovers that the fiber broke at the end of the second trench segment. Replace the optical path with a shorter interpretation.

```python
break_time = "2026-07-10"
break_distance = float(distance_edges[2])

resolved_path = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0].optical_paths[0]
broken_path = resolved_path.select(distance=(0.0, break_distance)).revise(
    start_time=break_time,
    end_time="2026-08-20",
    comment="Fiber break at the end of trench segment 2.",
)

inventory = inventory.replace(broken_path)

after_break = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0]

print(after_break.optical_paths[0].optical_length)
```

## Part 4: Add The Splice Repair Segment

On August 20, the break is repaired by splicing in a replacement segment. Replace the path again with the repaired interpretation.

```python
repair_start = trench_vertices[2]
repair_stop = np.array([235.0, 92.0, 0.0])
repair_length = float(np.linalg.norm(repair_stop - repair_start))
repaired_length = break_distance + repair_length

repair_fiber = inv.FiberSegment(
    name="Spliced replacement segment",
    optical_length=repair_length,
    fiber_type="single_mode",
    comment="Installed after July fiber break.",
)
repair_geometry = inv.Geometry(
    optical_length=repair_length,
    geometry_type="linear",
    coordinates=(tuple(repair_start), tuple(repair_stop)),
)
repair_coupling = inv.CouplingCondition(
    optical_length=repair_length,
    coupling_type="buried",
    deployment_type="trench",
    quality="repaired",
)
repair_annotation = inv.OpticalPathAnnotation(
    distance=(break_distance, repaired_length),
    label="splice_repair",
    category="repair",
)

repaired_path = broken_path.revise(
    start_time="2026-08-20",
    optical_length=repaired_length,
    coordinate_reference_system=crs,
    optical_components=(*broken_path.optical_components, repair_fiber),
    geometries=(*broken_path.geometries, repair_geometry),
    coupling_conditions=(*broken_path.coupling_conditions, repair_coupling),
    annotations=(*broken_path.annotations, repair_annotation),
).validate()

inventory = inventory.replace(repaired_path)

repaired = inventory.networks[0].fiber_arrays[0]

print(repaired.optical_paths[0].optical_length)
print(repaired.optical_paths[0].annotations[-1].label)
```

## Resolve Metadata For Patches

The same patch-level `data_source_id` resolves inventory context for patch enrichment.

```python
patch = dc.get_example_patch("random_das").update_attrs(
    data_source_id="XX.TRN.00.RAW",
)

september_patch = patch.update_coords(time_min="2026-09-01")

september_labels = september_patch.select(distance=(0, repaired_length)).add_inventory_coords(
    inventory,
    coords=("label",),
    on_boundary="ignore",
)

print(np.unique(september_labels.get_array("label")))
```
